assumptions: The developer has a supported browser, a terminal, and can run Docker locally.
No Kong product, Konnect account, personal access token, Enterprise license, Gateway instance, or decK configuration exists yet.

developer_goal: Create access to a Kong surface and reach the first observable proxied API success for a declared Kong product.

first_success.outcome: For the representative Kong Gateway getting-started candidate, a request proxied through one Gateway Service and Route returns HTTP 200.
first_success.observable_signal: The curl request through Kong Gateway to the configured /mock or /anything route returns HTTP 200.

route_selection.surface: Kong Developer homepage with Kong Gateway getting started as one documented candidate
route_selection.rule: Select a single product and deployment model only after official docs declare one platform-wide first success; otherwise leave peer products and Gateway deployment variants unresolved.

candidates.name: Kong Gateway getting started via Konnect hybrid
candidates.reason: The Gateway guide presents Konnect hybrid as a peer prerequisite path that requires Konnect registration, a personal access token, the quickstart script, and decK.

candidates.name: Kong Gateway getting started via self-managed Enterprise local Docker
candidates.reason: The same guide presents an on-prem Enterprise license plus local quickstart as a peer deployment variant with different credentials and no Konnect account.

candidates.name: Kong Konnect, Insomnia, AI Gateway, Event Gateway, Mesh, or other homepage products
candidates.reason: developer.konghq.com lists many applications and connectivity products without naming one company-wide first-success operation.

required_path.action: Choose a Kong product surface and, if the Konnect Gateway branch is selected, start Konnect registration.

required_fields.label: Kong product or application choice
required_fields.notes: The developer homepage asks what the developer wants to build across multiple products.

required_fields.label: Konnect identity fields for built-in or social signup
required_fields.notes: Konnect documents username/password or Google, GitHub, or Microsoft social login, but the exact fresh-trial signup fields are not fully listed in public docs.

required_path.observable_result: The developer has authenticated access to the selected Kong control plane or product.

required_path.action: On the Konnect Gateway branch, generate a Konnect personal access token.
required_path.observable_result: A Konnect PAT value is available to export as KONNECT_TOKEN.

required_path.action: Run the Kong quickstart script for the selected Konnect or Enterprise license path, install decK, create a Service and Route, and proxy a request.

required_fields.label: deck gateway apply Service and Route configuration

required_path.observable_result: decK can reach Gateway or Konnect, and the proxied request returns HTTP 200.

prerequisites.description: Docker is available for the Gateway quickstart container path.
decK 1.64.0 or newer and cURL are required by the Gateway getting-started tutorial.

external_gates.description: Self-managed Enterprise requires a Kong Gateway Enterprise license before the on-prem quickstart can run.
Konnect Plus and Enterprise users signing in with built-in authentication are prompted for MFA.

unavoidable_waits.description: The quickstart script must provision the Control Plane and local Data Plane before deck gateway ping succeeds.

platform_outcomes.description: The Konnect quickstart provisions a Control Plane named quickstart and a local Data Plane and prints session environment exports.
Kong Gateway proxies the configured Service and Route to the upstream and returns HTTP 200.

excluded.item: Reading Kong documentation indexes and product overview pages
excluded.reason: Documentation is evidence for route selection, not a counted product action.

excluded.item: Production Install page deployment outside the Docker quickstart
excluded.reason: The getting-started note points production installs elsewhere after the explored quickstart path.

sources.title: Kong Developer Documentation
evidence_supported: Multiple Kong products and applications on one homepage

sources.title: Get started with Kong Gateway
evidence_supported: Konnect hybrid versus self-managed Enterprise peer prerequisites

sources.title: Konnect platform authentication
evidence_supported: Google, GitHub, and Microsoft social login

uncertainties.question: Which Kong product should represent the company-wide first developer success?
uncertainties.impact: Product choice changes account model, credentials, install steps, configuration objects, and the completion signal.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A bounded workshop goal that names one Kong product and one first-success observable, or an official Kong statement of the default first developer journey.

uncertainties.question: If Kong Gateway is selected, is Konnect hybrid or self-managed Enterprise the comparison route?
uncertainties.impact: Konnect adds account and PAT work; Enterprise adds license entitlement and a different quickstart invocation.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A policy choice between the two peer prerequisites on the current Gateway getting-started page, plus a fresh-account walkthrough of Konnect trial signup fields.
